summary: TRIBE model is now loaded once per run via a persistent tribe_worker.py driven by the notebook. Unit + cross-process protocol tests pass and notebook static/syntax checks pass. Full GPU end-to-end integration on Colab is the remaining manual step (no GPU/checkpoint locally).

criteria.evidence: tribe_worker.py serve() loads the model once, reads line-delimited JSON jobs from stdin, emits one sentinel-prefixed result per job. Verified by test_worker_protocol.py (load count == 1 for 3 jobs) and test_worker_subprocess.py (real Popen pipes, statuses ['ready','ok','error','ok']).

Notebook now dispatches the TRIBE step through TribeWorkerSession created once before the per-file loop; both single-file and batch runs share the loop (job list length 1 or N). from_pretrained-once-per-run is guaranteed by session persistence + verified load-once in tests. Runtime confirmation on real checkpoint requires GPU (manual).

serve() wraps each job in try/except: a raising job yields status='error' with traceback and the loop continues (test_worker_protocol.py and test_worker_subprocess.py). Notebook raises RuntimeError on non-ok result, caught by the existing per-file except -> batch_failures.json + continue.

TribeWorkerCrashed raised when the worker process dies (start/run_job detect poll()!=None). Notebook lazily restarts a dead session on the next video, increments tribe_worker_restarts, and aborts that video after TRIBE_WORKER_MAX_RESTARTS. Present in notebook static checks. Real crash/OOM recovery requires GPU (manual).

The existing 'if can_reuse_tribe:' cache+settings check still precedes dispatch; the worker is created only inside the else (needs-TRIBE) branch, so it starts lazily on the first video that actually needs TRIBE. Notebook static check confirms structure.

gr.Checkbox 'Persistent TRIBE worker (load model once per run)' (default True) wired into inputs and the analyze_video signature; 'if use_persistent_worker:' selects worker, else the unchanged legacy run_command_stream(tribe_cmd) path (both branches present in static checks).

TribeWorkerSession._drain_nowait routes sentinel-prefixed lines to the result parser and all other worker stdout/stderr lines to log_lines (streamed to gradio log + gradio_last_run.log). Tests confirm non-prefixed lines are not mis-parsed as results.

run_tribe_v2 keeps its original signature and now delegates to load_tribe_model + process_input_with_model; main() is unchanged. py_compile passes. `--help` cannot run locally only because heavy deps (nibabel etc.) are not installed in this environment; the import line is unchanged from before.

tribe_worker.py starts with '# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-'. Secrets are never on the worker command line (worker_cmd has only checkpoint/cache-dir/device) or in result lines (status/paths only); OPENROUTER_API_KEY/HF_TOKEN flow via inherited env as before.

manual_followups: Run a 2-video batch on Colab GPU with one intentionally broken input; confirm a single from_pretrained load in gradio_last_run.log, 1 processed + 1 failed in batch_failures.json, and a correct combined ZIP.
Force a CUDA OOM to confirm worker restart-on-crash and bounded restarts.
